event_blocks.summary: Upbeat, slightly tense background music accompanies a female voiceover stating, "Fast food is everywhere," as a young child with curly hair in a high chair eats fries with a fork, a purple sippy cup and McDonald’s box on the tray. The scene shifts to an infant in a blue bib being spoon-fed, then a young boy in a blue sweater on a black leather couch, who, according to a male voice, likes KFC, chicken nuggets, kebabs, fish and chips, and "fizzy pop," consuming "probably on average say six a day," with the speaker admitting, "I've got no control of what he eats." A woman with dark hair and a black top tells the camera, "Anything that's bad for you, he'll eat," and the voiceover continues, "Our kids just can't get enough of it." Visuals include a child in a gray sweater being fed a burger in a car, another child in a high chair with fries and a chicken nugget, and a child in a yellow hoodie holding a red Coca-Cola bottle in a grocery aisle. A woman in a red polka-dot top speaks in a kitchen, followed by a close-up of a hand reaching into a refrigerator filled with snacks and alcohol. The woman in the black top lists health consequences: "tooth decay, iron deficiency anemia, rickets even," and later, "They are a recipe for heart disease, diabetes." A child's voice calls out "Nona!" as the voiceover warns, "It's a ticking time bomb of massive health problems." The woman in the black top is seen being comforted on a couch by another woman in a colorful scarf. The video returns to a child in a high chair with a blue bib being spoon-fed, and a group eating around a table, with a woman in a red top reaching for food. The voiceover concludes, "We meet three families with three different fast food problems," ending with a close-up of a plate of fried food, possibly fish and chips, with a hand dipping a piece into ketchup.

event_blocks.summary: In a car, a child named Cuba, with curly hair and wearing a gray sweater, is fed a burger from a white takeout container by an adult's hand, while another child sits beside them; Simon, the father, a man in a black jacket, is heard stating he takes the kids to McDonald's on Friday and Saturday. Transitioning to a kitchen, Simon describes Cuba's "very big cute smile," and after Cuba exclaims "It's hot," Simon notes, "It's burning, he needs some sauce." The family moves to the kitchen where Sam, Cuba's mum, in a blue and white striped shirt, cooks while a young girl in a pink top watches; later, Sam, now in a red polka-dot top, feeds Cuba fries in a high chair with a red patterned backrest, discussing her pasta, which she feels is like "shop-bought pasta" and "nobody eats it," to which Simon replies "No," amidst laughter. At the dining table, Simon holds a baby in a blue bib, and Cuba eats fries. Sam, in the striped shirt, prepares food at the counter and then cleans by throwing trash into a bin. In the living room, Sam vacuums the carpet while Cuba plays with a toy vacuum. The video concludes with Sam folding clothes on a wooden table, with a child in a blue cardigan nearby, as background music plays and the narrator states Sam works two days a week as a banking consultant and spends the other five "meticulously" cleaning her home. In a separate scene, a woman in a blue and white striped shirt cleans as a young child with curly hair, wearing a light blue vest, plays with a toy hammer on a glass table, with a narrator commenting on avoiding dishes by eating out. At the kitchen sink, the woman loads a dish rack while Cuba explores cabinets, pulling out a green container, and stands by the open cabinet holding a small object as the woman crouches nearby; the narrator mentions that "Cuba and sisters Cleo and Shay also have free rein to plunder the sweetie cupboard." A man in a dark sweater speaks directly to the camera, stating the child eats "at least 30 times for the day" and he has "no control of what he eats." Cuba, in a high chair, eats fries with a fork, and the narrator remarks that "whilst the girls have learned self-control, Cuba hasn't," as the child makes babbling sounds. Later, Cuba is seen in a crib, standing and holding a blue pacifier, while a woman in a black top with a red belt interacts with them; the narrator describes Cuba as knocking "all the fast food back with an unbelievable amount of cola," up to "six cans a day," declaring "it's out of control" as the child laughs. A woman in a white top sits on a couch, laughing and gesturing, as the narrator adds that "Cuba can't sleep and Sam is now worried about his health." The woman's voice is heard saying she's "totally conscious of it now" and needs to "be reined back in and just start from scratch." Cuba is then seen in the crib, holding a bottle and pacifier, before ending with the child looking directly at the camera with a curious expression.

raw_segments: The video opens with a woman in a white top and black feathered shawl sitting contemplatively on a plush brown couch, stating, "I just don't know where to start really, I think with her." The scene shifts to a kitchen where a man in a red sports jersey, identified by a narrator as Simon, feeds a young child with curly hair, named Sam, who is seated in a high chair eating fries. The narrator comments, "Sam is prepared for change. But is Simon?" Simon, now in a dark hoodie, speaks directly to the camera, admitting, "I'm not too sure about the fast food. Quite enjoy that. I think he enjoys that. So I wouldn't like to stop too much of that." Upbeat, slightly jazzy background music begins as the video transitions to an outdoor suburban street with parked cars and terraced houses, where a pigeon perches on a TV antenna. A narrator introduces "Jane Imperato," a woman in a red top and black jacket, carrying a blue bag and a black container, as she walks toward a house and enters through the front door. Inside, Jane, described as a "hugely experienced family health visitor" who has "spent 20 years coercing families onto healthy diets," is seen speaking to the camera, asserting, "I think that I am reasonably firm. I think I'm a good parent... to my parents." She then interacts with a baby, identified as Cuba, in a blue bib, feeding him with a spoon. Jane plays with colorful building blocks on the floor with Cuba, saying "Hi there" and "You're doing it" as the baby coos, and later exclaiming, "Look what we've got here. Let's have a look at those. Wow, those are pretty, aren't they?" while Cuba makes happy sounds. Another woman in a black top sits nearby. The two women then sit on the couch, engaging in conversation in the cozy, softly lit living room, with Cuba playing on the floor between them. The narrator states, "Cuba has no choice in what he eats. To crack his problem, Jane will have to crack the whole family." Jane addresses the other woman, asking, "Tell me all about what's been happening... and what you feel your main issues are."

The video opens in a cozy living room with soft lighting and patterned wallpaper, where the Woman in black top and the Woman in colorful scarf are seated on a plush couch, engaged in a serious, emotionally charged conversation. The Woman in black top, appearing distressed with crossed arms, expresses deep concern in a worried tone, stating that someone "is not eating properly" and that "His diet at the moment, Sam, is bad. Worse than bad, it's dreadful." She later voices her main concern that "he might be anemic." The Woman in colorful scarf, gesturing empathetically, offers comfort while calmly questioning, "How many takeaways do you have in a week?" and then explaining the health risks of such a diet, noting that takeaways are "high in fats and salt and sugar" and "a recipe for heart disease, diabetes, cancer." Amidst their discussion, a baby, identified by a narrator as "Cuba," dressed in a white shirt, gray vest, and blue jeans, plays with colorful building blocks on the carpet, occasionally looking up. The narrator reveals that "Cuba is consuming 3,200 calories a day, 700 more than is healthy for a grown man," and that "in his short 19-month life, Cuba's diet may already be taking its toll." The baby is later seen lying calmly on the couch with a blue pacifier. The scene briefly shifts to a dining area where a young child with curly hair, wearing a gray shirt, eats fries in a high chair, while another child in a green shirt sits at the table, providing moments of innocence. The camera then returns to the living room, capturing the baby resting and the two women continuing their conversation, with the Woman in black top now looking more reflective, as the serious discussion about health and diet unfolds.

The video opens with a close-up of Cuba, an infant in a gray vest, white shirt, and blue jeans, lying on a plush brown couch with a blue pacifier in his mouth. A soft, slightly melancholic background music begins as a female narrator explains, "In the UK, one in eight toddlers are now iron deficient. This can, in the worst cases, slow a child's development," adding that "Cuba will need a blood test to see if he's anemic." The camera then pulls back to reveal a living room setting where Jane, a woman with dark hair in a black top, is seated on a couch, conversing with another woman in a colorful scarf and dark top. The woman in the scarf asks, "How long's it been going on?" to which a voice (likely Jane's) replies, "Frankly as long as he's been born." The narrator then introduces Jane, stating, "Right now, Jane lays down the ground rules for the whole family." The scene transitions to Cuba sitting on a beige carpet, playing with colorful building blocks, while the woman in the colorful scarf interacts with him, offering a toy. A detailed shot of a large, ornate dollhouse is shown before the setting shifts to a kitchen. Here, Sam, a man in a black hoodie with neon green drawstrings, holds Cuba, who is then passed between Sam and Jane. Jane is heard asserting, "We need to start cooking in this household. I want them on three meals a day." Sam interjects, "Sam, cooking," and Jane clarifies, "It's not, no, it's not all about Sam. I I I need you to back Sam up. When you're looking after the children, I need you to cook for them." Sam asks, "Are you against this?" and Jane responds, "Yes, you are. You can cook, Sam. You can cook." Sam replies, "It's just getting the time yourself, obviously," with Jane adding, "Yeah, working from home." Cuba is then placed on a kitchen counter, leaning over a metal bowl. The woman in the colorful scarf reappears, holding a small orange fruit, and interacts with Cuba, asking Jane, "And were you saying that you didn't like the mess involved in cooking?" to which Jane replies, "Oh, the mess he makes, it's just irritating." The atmosphere throughout is warm and familial, with soft lighting and a focus on nurturing interactions between the adults and Cuba, who remains the central figure, consistently wearing the same outfit and blue pacifier.

event_blocks.summary: In a warmly lit home, Gareth, a man in a black shirt, pours Coca-Cola from a red bottle into a glass at a wooden dining table, while Kara, a woman in a cream-colored sweater, eats vegetables and potatoes from a bowl. Their young child, Michael, in a blue and gray striped shirt and jeans, sits in a wooden high chair eating from a white bowl. Michael becomes distressed, crying loudly, whimpering, and pushing his bowl away. Kara attempts to comfort him, saying "Yes. Eat your dinner," and "Come on," while Gareth also tries to soothe him, calling out "Michael, Michael." A narrator explains that "the more they try and control his eating... the more Michael rebels." Kara firmly tells Michael, "No, you don't do that. You don't do that," and Gareth adds, "Very naughty." Gareth then stands, gently interacts with the crying Michael, and lifts him from the high chair, carrying him into a hallway as Michael continues to cry. A close-up shows Kara's concerned face. Michael is then seen standing in a living room, still crying amidst toys and a play tent. Gareth reappears, picks up Michael again, and walks down the hallway, with Kara following. Kara, sounding exasperated, comments, "This is what it's like every mealtime," and later reflects that the situation is "affecting Kara and Gareth's relationship," causing them to "be stressed each other because of what's going on and having to clean up all the food." She concludes, "it's a stressful time," before firmly telling Michael, "Michael, sit down, please!"

event_blocks.summary: Kara, the Woman with dark hair and black top, expresses her embarrassment about her son's unhealthy eating habits in a dimly lit room, underscored by upbeat music. A framed photograph of a woman holding a baby and a yellow toy on a mantelpiece are shown, followed by a sunset landscape. The narrator introduces Kara and Gareth's meeting with Catherine, the Woman in red long-sleeve top, a clinical psychologist, who is seen walking down a suburban street at dusk and entering their home. Catherine is greeted at the front door of house by a child's voice and enters a living room with a play tent and toys, where Michael, the Child in red outfit, is on the floor. Catherine explains to Kara, seated on a black leather couch, that she will observe Michael's mealtime. The scene shifts to the wooden dining table where Catherine serves food, Kara sits beside her, and Michael eats in his high chair, accompanied by the Man in striped shirt serving drinks. Amidst the clattering of dishes, Kara offers 'Carrot?' Later, in the same warmly lit living room, Michael makes sounds as he eats, prompting Kara to encourage him to eat his food. Gareth also interacts with Michael, while Catherine, the Woman in purple top, observes from the black leather couch. As Michael becomes fussy, Kara lifts him from the high chair and Catherine comforts him on the couch, offering to let him watch his program. The narrator explains that Kara and Gareth often give in to Michael's demands, letting him eat on the sofa. Catherine begins her consultation, discussing Michael's weaning process and the importance of introducing new tastes and textures.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a close-up of the Woman with dark hair and black top, later identified as Kara, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit room, expressing her deep embarrassment as a mum about giving her son sweets, crisps, and chocolate because he refuses healthy food. Upbeat, slightly dramatic background music begins as the scene transitions to a still image of a framed photograph showing a smiling woman holding a baby on a mantelpiece, alongside a small yellow toy, evoking family memories. This is followed by a serene sunset shot, casting a warm orange glow. A narrator then states that 'Kara and Gareth are desperate for help,' and are meeting Dr. Catherine Dendy, ex-head of feeding at Great Ormond Street Children's Hospital. The scene shifts to a suburban street at dusk, where the Woman in red long-sleeve top, revealed to be Catherine, walks past a 'Fish & Chips' shop and parked cars before entering a warmly lit home. A doorbell rings, and Catherine is greeted by a child's voice. She introduces herself as 'Catherine,' and a man's voice responds, 'Nice to meet you.' The camera follows Catherine into a cozy living room filled with toys and a play tent, where a baby, referred to as Michael, in a red outfit lies on the floor, reaching for a colorful book. The narrator explains Catherine's role as a clinical psychologist. Catherine then gestures expressively as she speaks to Kara, who is seated on a black leather couch, explaining she's there 'to see Michael having a meal' and to 'experience with you what happens,' asking them to 'do what you would normally do.' Kara responds with an affirmative sound. The narrator states Catherine observes a typical mealtime. The final scene shows a family meal at a wooden dining table, with Catherine serving food, Kara seated beside her, and Michael, the child in a high chair, eating and making sounds, while a man in a striped shirt serves drinks. Kara asks, 'Carrot?' amidst the clattering of dishes.

The scene opens in a warmly lit, lived-in living room, where a family is sharing a meal around a wooden dining table. A young child, identified as Michael, in a red long-sleeve shirt, is seated, making soft 'goo-goo' sounds and a distinct 'clink' as he reaches into a bowl of food. A woman with dark hair and a black top, later identified as Karen, sits beside him, gently prompting him with 'Tato' and 'Stop playing with your food. Put it in your mouth, it's yummy!' as he babbles and makes playful noises. A man in a striped shirt, Gareth, is also at the table, eating and interacting, occasionally saying 'No, no' to Michael's actions. In the background, a woman with short blonde hair in a purple top, Catherine, observes from a black leather couch. As Michael becomes fussy, making louder 'ah!' sounds and babbling, Karen lifts him from the high chair and carries him to the couch, where Catherine comforts him with a 'Shh!' and asks, 'Do you want to watch your program?' Michael is then placed on the couch, continuing to eat from a bowl while being watched over. A narrator's voice explains, 'As usual, Karen and Gareth end up giving in to Michael's demands and put him in his favorite spot on the sofa.' Catherine then begins a consultation, stating, 'It seems to me that what's happened to Michael is that this really, really important stage of weaning and building up the new tastes and the new textures...' The scene captures intimate family dynamics, focusing on Michael's needs and the adults' responsive care.

event_blocks.summary: A woman with dark hair and a black top and a man in a checkered shirt converse on a black leather couch, with the man's arm around her. A woman in a purple top expresses concern about someone feeling anxious and describes a feeding cycle, to which the man interjects about it being natural. The woman in purple then recalls the phrase "Don't play with your food, Michael," advocating for children to play with their food, with table manners to follow later, while a young child in a red outfit eats from a green bowl on the couch. The scene shifts to a nighttime house exterior with a crescent moon, then an arched bridge over a river and a white seagull. During the day, a child in a blue sweater sits on the porch of a brick house. Inside, a woman in a white tank top offers garlic bread to the child, who refuses, and she then offers one piece. A narrator states Harley Evans, aged three, has never had a home-cooked healthy meal. A woman in a black coat carries a yellow container of fries, and inside, the child drinks a milkshake and eats fries while the woman eats pizza. The narrator details Harley's weekly diet of doner meat, fried chicken, chips, frozen pizzas, nuggets, and fizzy drinks, explaining it's because he eats what his mum eats. The woman, Taylor, with purple hair, interacts with Harley near a refrigerator. Taylor, preferring fast food for its speed and taste, orders kebabs and KFC. A high chair and play tent are visible. The narrator reveals Taylor had Harley at 15. Later, Baby Tiana, in a red outfit, is introduced as a single mum's daughter. Woman Tiana, in a white tank top and black feathered shawl, sits with Young boy Harley, eating pizza and fries, as Tiana warns him to be careful. Taylor, with purple hair, expresses concern about Harley's hyperactivity due to his diet. In a grocery store, Taylor pushes a shopping cart with Harley, who spots chocolate and asks for pizza. Taylor interacts with her mother, Mandy, in a red jacket, who expresses disapproval of the food choices. The shopping cart contains a "TOPPED TO THE EDGE" pizza box and a "MEGA-PARTY" Haribo candy box. In another grocery store scene, the Woman in red jacket and the Woman with purple hair shop, with the boy, Harley, in the cart asking for pasta and snacks. The Woman with purple hair denies him snacks and asks for chocolate, which he refuses. In a kitchen, Harley eats fries from a yellow container while the Woman in red jacket holds a pizza box. The Woman with purple hair expresses concern about Taylor's diet and the risk of obesity, and an unseen male voice mentions exercise. The Woman with purple hair emphasizes Harley's diet needs to change to avoid it becoming the norm. Harley is seen eating pizza and fries, followed by an outdoor shot of pink and red children's bicycles against a brick wall.

raw_segments: The video opens in a cozy living room, focusing on a woman with dark hair in a black top and a man in a checkered shirt, seated intimately on a black leather couch. They share a relaxed conversation, with the man's arm around her. The scene then cuts to a woman with short blonde hair in a purple top, also on the couch, speaking with a concerned expression. She is heard discussing how someone has "been interrupted" and "been feeling really, really anxious about it," continuing to explain that "if he starts eating something, you say great, and you give him more of it," describing it as "like a circle." She emphasizes that "even if he's like the slightest bit, something's not right," and expresses a feeling of "oh, there's something wrong with him." The man briefly interjects with "Yeah, yes, inside," and "It's natural though, isn't it?" The woman in purple top continues, stating that "he is keen to put his hands into things and try them out." The camera returns to the couple, where the woman laughs and gestures playfully, and the man smiles, indicating a lighthearted moment. The woman in purple top reappears, speaking with a serious and reflective tone, recalling a phrase: "Don't play with your food, Michael." She elaborates, "I know we're all brought up not to play with our food," to which the man agrees, "That's right, yeah." She then asserts, "actually, what we want him to do is play with his food," adding that "his table manners will come later." A brief cut shows a young child in a red long-sleeve shirt quietly eating from a green bowl on the couch. The scene shifts back to the couple, now laughing and interacting warmly, with the woman leaning into the man. The woman in purple top concludes her thought with a more animated expression, as the camera pans slightly to reveal a radiator and a window ledge adorned with framed family photos, reinforcing the domestic and intimate atmosphere.

The video opens with a close-up of a man in a checkered shirt and a woman in a black top, seated closely on a black leather couch, smiling and engaging in conversation. The scene then shifts to a woman in a purple top, seated on a wooden chair, speaking animatedly about helping someone get used to fruit and veg, and doing it in a fun way. The man in the checkered shirt responds with agreement, saying, "Hopefully, yeah," and "That'd be great, wouldn't it, yeah." The camera returns to the couple on the couch, with the man's arm around the woman, both smiling, before panning to a wider view of the living room, revealing a child in a blue sweater on the couch, a play tent, and scattered toys, indicating a family-friendly space. A female narrator's voice then introduces background music, explaining that Catherine will refer Michael to a children's feeding expert, Lucy Thomas, for practical support, and work with Cara and Gareth on their anxieties. The visuals transition to a nighttime exterior of a house with a lit window, followed by a crescent moon in the dark sky, accompanied by the sound of a car driving. An arched bridge over a river with passing vehicles is shown, then a white seagull perched on a railing. The scene cuts to a daytime exterior of a brick house with the child in the blue sweater sitting on the porch. Inside, a woman in a white tank top is seen opening a pizza box on the black leather couch, while the child eats a slice. The woman's voice is heard asking the child, "Right, how many pieces of garlic bread do you want, mate?" and offering, "I'll give you two then to start off with just in case you want some more," to which the child responds with a clear "No!" The woman then concedes, "Just one then." The female narrator's voice returns, stating, "Harley Evans lives in Runcorn. There's no battle here over fruit and veg. He's reached the grand old age of three without his mum ever having cooked him a healthy meal." The scene shifts to a street view where a woman in a black coat carries a yellow container of fries. Back inside, the woman in the white tank top hands a drink to the child, who is now eating fries from the yellow container, while she continues to eat pizza. The child's voice is heard saying, "A milkshake," and "And look, come with it," followed by the woman's voice saying, "It's yellow," and the child responding, "Banana," to which the woman confirms, "Yeah." The atmosphere is casual and domestic, focusing on family meals and interactions in a lived-in home environment.

The segment opens in a cozy, lived-in home with warm, natural lighting, where a young boy, later identified as Harley, in a blue sweater sits on a black leather couch, actively eating pizza and fries from a red and white checkered pizza box. He babbles and shouts 'Naa, nee, ye-ke!' and later exclaims 'Wow!' as he enjoys his meal, also seen holding a blue sippy cup and a small toy. A male narrator with a British accent details a diet consisting of two portions of doner meat, eight pieces of fried chicken, four portions of chips, two frozen pizzas, and 20 frozen nuggets weekly, along with 24 glasses of fizzy drink, equating to two half-kilo packets of sugar. The narrator explains this diet is because 'he's eating what his mum eats.' The scene shifts briefly to a kitchen where a woman with purple hair interacts with Harley near a refrigerator adorned with colorful magnetic letters and numbers. Back in the living room, a woman, later identified as Taylor, with dark hair, wearing a white tank top and a black feathered shawl, sits on the same black leather couch. She opens the pizza box and eats a slice while Harley, energetic and curious, plays nearby, climbing on the couch and exploring the space. Taylor, speaking with a British accent, discusses her preference for fast food, stating, 'Can I have a small doner meat with chips, with cheese and mayonnaise please?' and explaining it's 'quicker, easier... Someone's made it for you, you don't have to stand around waiting for it to cook... it tastes nice... Cuz they're not a bother to cook.' She adds that she 'usually order[s] a kebab every night,' sometimes KFC. The setting also reveals a high chair with a red patterned backrest and a play tent in the background. The narrator concludes by revealing, 'Team mum Taylor had Harley when she was just 15.'

The video opens with a close-up of Baby Tiana, an infant in a red outfit, sitting on a black leather couch with wide, curious eyes, whimpering softly as a narrator introduces her as a single mum's daughter. The scene then widens to show Woman Tiana, in a white tank top and black feathered shawl, seated on the couch with her son, Young boy Harley, who is eating pizza and fries. Tiana gestures animatedly, telling Harley to 'Be careful, you,' as the narrator explains that Tiana is frazzled and Harley is becoming more of a handful. The camera cuts to a kitchen where Woman Taylor, with purple hair, holds a baby in a blue bib near a refrigerator adorned with magnetic letters. Taylor, identified by the narrator as frazzled, expresses her concern that Harley is 'more hyperactive than he has been' due to the food he's eating. The scene transitions to a grocery store, where Taylor pushes a shopping cart with Harley, now in a blue jacket, seated inside. Upbeat background music plays as Harley excitedly exclaims, 'I can see the chocolate!' Taylor suggests, 'Should we go and get some pizza?' and asks if he wants 'pepperoni or cheese,' to which Harley responds, 'I want one of them. Peas. I like peas.' Taylor teases him, 'You've never tasted a pea, have you, Harley?' as they interact with Woman Mandy, Taylor's mum, in a red jacket, who is browsing the produce section and comments, 'I don't like that.' The narrator reveals Mandy's concerns about what Taylor feeds Harley and herself. The video concludes with a close-up of the shopping cart filled with items like a 'TOPPED TO THE EDGE' pizza box and a 'MEGA-PARTY' Haribo candy box, as Mandy remarks, 'You've missed all the good food outside.'

The video opens in a brightly lit grocery store where the Woman in red jacket and the Woman with purple hair are shopping. The Woman with purple hair pushes a shopping cart containing the Young boy in blue sweater, who, identified by his voice, says "Pasta" and "I want some shopping" while reaching for a yellow bag of snacks. The Woman with purple hair, speaking with a clear voice, responds, "No, we don't like it. Takes ages to make," and later asks the boy, "Can I have some of your chocolate, please?" to which he firmly replies "No" twice, even after she prompts "A little bite." The scene transitions to a home kitchen where the Woman with purple hair, now in a gray blazer, stands beside the Young boy in blue sweater (Harley), who is sitting on a counter eating fries from a yellow container. The Woman in red jacket, now in a black top, holds a pizza box, and the Woman with purple hair continues to speak, expressing concern about Harley's diet, stating, "Last time Taylor had a vegetable, it was from the kebab shop, deep-fried... If she carries on the way she is, she's gonna end up obese." She adds, "No, but if you carry on with your takeaways, that's how it's gonna go," accompanied by rustling sounds, possibly from the pizza box or snack bag. An unseen male voice interjects with "Exercise," prompting the Woman with purple hair to ask, "What, how do you exercise?" The man replies, "We're allowed after holiday," and the Woman with purple hair mentions "To the shop," while Harley's voice is heard saying "To buy a bottle" amidst more rustling. The Woman with purple hair then concludes, "And a packet of what's it's, that's it," followed by the man repeating "Exercise" and Harley's distinct yelling/laughing. The Woman with purple hair then emphasizes, "Harley's diet really does need to change now because otherwise that's, he will think it's the norm to eat what Taylor's giving him. It's just not healthy." The boy is seen eating pizza from a red and white checkered box, followed by a close-up of him eating fries, before the video cuts to two children's bicycles outdoors, one pink and one red, leaning against a brick wall.

event_blocks.summary: A small, fluffy black and white dog barks energetically behind a rusted metal gate in a garden as somber instrumental music begins, transitioning to a cozy living room where a woman with dark hair and a zebra-print headband shares fries and dipping sauce from a takeaway box with a young boy in a blue sweater on a black leather couch. A narrator states that "Taylor should know more than most what her diet could be doing to Harley," noting that "Last year, she suffered a heart attack." The camera focuses on the fries and sauce, emphasizing the intimate moment. event_blocks.summary: A woman, Taylor, visits community dietitian Haley Kooter, who specializes in pediatric nutrition, at her home with floral wallpaper. Haley, wearing a white hoodie with a 'Smile' graphic, and Taylor, in a red long-sleeve top, discuss the dietary habits of a young boy, Harley, who is identified as Taylor's son. Haley measures Harley's height with a wooden and blue device, noting his BMI is above average and expressing concern over his high consumption of sugary cola and takeaways. During a collaborative cooking session in a dining area with floral wallpaper and teal curtains, Harley, wearing a red plaid shirt and holding a green sippy cup, mixes ingredients in a glass bowl under the guidance of Haley and Taylor. Despite being told he needs no more sugar, Harley expresses a desire for more, and Taylor reveals he is consuming almost four times the recommended daily sugar intake for his age, and later states he is having 500 grams in one day, which is ten times the recommended amount for an adult. A printed recipe sheet and a bottle of milk are visible on the table. An interview segment features a woman in a red polka-dot top warning about a "junk food generation" leading to numerous health problems, including tooth decay, constipation, and rickets, all attributed to an unbalanced diet. raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a baby in a pink and white checkered bouncer, smiling and playing with a colorful toy, as a child's voice exclaims "Look!". A female narrator then introduces a "tragedy that Maddie hopes can be avoided if Taylor takes action right now," as the scene widens to show a woman with dark hair and a zebra-print headband sharing fries with a young boy in a blue shirt on a black leather couch. Upbeat acoustic guitar music begins as the setting shifts to an outdoor suburban street with terraced brick houses. A woman, identified as Taylor, in a red long-sleeve top and black jacket, arrives at a house with a blue bag. She is greeted at the red-framed front door by a woman in a white hoodie with 'Smile' printed on it, who introduces herself as Haley, the dietitian, saying "Hi, Taylor, I'm Haley, I'm the dietitian." Taylor responds, "Hello, come in. Hi, I've come to see you." The narrator explains that Taylor is seeing community dietitian Haley Kooter, who specializes in pediatric nutrition. Inside the living room with floral wallpaper, Taylor places her bag down and interacts with a young boy in a red plaid shirt. Haley measures the boy's height using a wooden and blue height-measuring device, asking him, "Can you see that number? That shows you what a big boy you are," followed by a child's giggle. A female narrator highlights that "One in five children in the UK are now overweight by the age of five." The scene cuts to an interview-style shot of a woman in a red polka-dot top speaking in front of a cityscape window, warning that "A junk food generation is a ticking time bomb for a really worrying amount of problems." She continues, describing "a lot of overweight and obese children at the moment," and health issues like "tooth decay, constipation, rickets even, from lack of vitamin D," all "caused by an unbalanced diet." The video returns to the living room where Taylor continues to engage with the boy, adjusting the height-measuring device, before ending with a wide shot of the row of terraced houses as the music fades.

The video opens in a cozy living room, where Hailey, a woman with vibrant purple hair wearing a white hoodie with a pink 'Smile' graphic, sits on a brown leather couch with teal cushions, discussing a child's diet. She states, "according to this BMI, it is above average, and we're gonna need to watch his food intake, really," and mentions, "These takeaways, um, he's hardly having as well." Beside her, a woman with dark hair and a dark blue top, seated on the same couch, takes notes. She adds, "Everything I've got, he wants," and later admits, "I don't think I've ever said he can't, I just give it to him," when Hailey asks if she thinks the boy copies her. A young boy with light hair, wearing a red and black plaid shirt, sits between them, holding a pacifier and looking around curiously. Upbeat, slightly jazzy background music begins as a narrator identifies Hailey as being "most concerned about the enormous amount of sugary cola that Harley is consuming," confirming the boy's name is Harley. The scene shifts to a close-up of Harley’s feet in green and white sneakers as he stands on a wooden floor, then to a low-angle shot from inside a refrigerator as he reaches for a snack, with a bottle of alcohol and colorful containers visible. Harley is then seen on a black leather couch, drinking from a red KFC cup, with the woman with dark hair smiling beside him. The setting transitions to a dining area with floral wallpaper and green curtains, where Harley stands at a wooden table with black placemats. Hailey and the woman with dark hair prepare a mixture in a glass bowl, adding flour and a dark liquid. Harley, asking "Can I have it all?" and "Can I have a little bit more?" as he reaches into the bowl, mixes the contents with his hand, guided by the women. Hailey instructs him, "Keep going," while the woman with dark hair encourages, "Go for it, Harley knows," as sounds of mixing accompany the activity. The atmosphere is warm and familial, with the women nurturing Harley's participation in the playful cooking activity.

In a warmly lit dining room adorned with floral wallpaper and teal curtains, a collaborative cooking session unfolds. The Woman with dark hair and black top, wearing a dark blue top, guides the Young boy in red plaid shirt, initially addressed as "Mr. Harley," as she pours flour from a glass bowl onto a digital scale. She notes, "It's about 150 grams there," while the boy, holding a green sippy cup, interjects, "I need that sugar." She firmly responds, "No." The Woman with purple hair, in a white hoodie with a pink 'Smile' graphic, observes and occasionally assists, using a spoon to add ingredients. Light, slightly melancholic background music begins as a narrator's voice explains that "Harley is having almost four times the amount of sugar that is recommended for his age," adding that "he's just a mini version of his mum." The Woman with dark hair then asks the boy about his sugar intake, to which he replies, "No, I think it would be more." She encourages him to show how much more, and he exclaims, "Jeez." As the boy becomes more involved, reaching into the bowl and mixing with his hands, he states, "After this, I, I want to go again." The Woman with dark hair responds, "Okay," and then tells him, "I'm afraid you're having a bit more than that." She asks, "Should we try again?" and the Woman with purple hair chimes in with "Yeah?" The Woman with dark hair emphasizes, "It's just a day. This is in one day." The boy asks, "And you want me to keep going?" to which she replies, "Keep going," prompting an "Oh my god" from him. Sounds of pouring and mixing accompany the activity. The Woman with dark hair then declares, "That's 500 grams, Taylor, in one day," now referring to the boy as "Taylor." The narrator's voice concludes, "Taylor is having ten times the recommended daily amount for a grown..." A bottle of milk and a printed recipe sheet are visible on the table, underscoring the structured nature of the cooking. The camera occasionally zooms in on the bowl, highlighting the hands-on activity within the cozy, domestic setting.

The Woman with purple hair, wearing a white hoodie with 'Smile' printed in pink, agrees that Hailey's description fits her, attributing her eating habits to laziness and expressing a desire to eat healthily if it leads to saving money and acquiring more. Across from her, the Woman with purple hair, wearing a white hoodie with 'Smile' printed in pink, observes attentively, smiling and confirming that Hailey's description "describes me." Hailey then asks why she eats "like this," to which the Woman with purple hair attributes it to "laziness," expressing interest in eating "healthily" if it means she "could save money and get more stuff." The table is set with a bottle of milk, a green sippy cup, and a printed recipe sheet, all within a cozy domestic room featuring floral wallpaper, teal curtains, and framed artwork of green flowers. The camera alternates between close-ups of the flour and wider shots of their interaction before shifting to a young boy, Harley, with light hair, wearing a blue cardigan, sitting on a brown leather couch with teal cushions, focused on a blue toy. The scene then transitions to an outdoor suburban street at dusk, showing terraced brick houses and a large arched bridge over a river, as the Woman with purple hair is seen standing outside a house with a red-framed front door, smiling and speaking with resolve. event_blocks.summary: A woman, Sam Fish, discusses the disconnect between food and health, noting how individuals are unaware of the detrimental effects of sugar, saturated fats, and salt on their well-being, as melancholic music begins. Transitioning to a car interior, Sam drives while a narrator explains her struggle to improve her son Cuba's nutrition, highlighting the risk of anemia and developmental delays. Cuba, a young boy in a gray puffer jacket, sits in a car seat, looking around. Sam's husband, Simon, expresses guilt over Cuba's diet and the need for a blood test due to potential anemia. Sam then takes Cuba to Meadow Sure Start Children’s Centre, where Cuba plays with a bead maze toy. Inside a treatment room, a nurse, identified as a woman in a white uniform, gently administers a procedure to Cuba, who remains calm with a pacifier, while Sam, now in a leopard-print coat, observes. Later, in a clinical setting, the woman in the white top administers an injection to Cuba, who cries distressfully, comforted by Sam, now wearing a leopard-print coat. After the procedure, Sam carries the crying Cuba out of the room, passing signs for 'Dr. R.K. Tiwari Reception' and 'Cardiff & The Vale'. Sam, narrating, reflects on the traumatic experience for both herself and Cuba, awaiting results in a couple of weeks. The scene shifts to a bright, modern kitchen with white cabinetry where Sam, in a gray sleeveless sweater, discusses food items with a child. Two young girls are briefly shown in another kitchen setting before returning to Sam, who is preparing fresh meals. Simon, in a black polo shirt, unpacks a black bag containing a red container and a white tray onto a kitchen island. Sam confronts Simon about his lack of effort with the children's diet, expressing her frustration and disappointment. Simon admits to enjoying takeaway, leading to an argument about dietary responsibility. Gentle, reflective music plays as Sam continues meal preparation.

raw_segments: The video opens with a woman with shoulder-length brown hair, wearing a black coat and a light gray scarf, standing outdoors in a foggy, overcast environment. She speaks directly to the camera in a calm, reflective voice, discussing how people have "forgotten that food and health are connected," and how someone has "no idea that the sugar, the fat, the saturated fats, the salt that she's putting in her body... is having any effect whatsoever on her health." Somber background music begins as the scene transitions to a moving vehicle shot, showing a blur of residential buildings and trees. Inside the car, the same woman is seen driving, focused on the road, her reflection visible in the rearview mirror. A female narrator's voice explains, "In Cardiff, Sam Fish is facing a huge struggle to get Cuba on the right nutritional track," adding that "His current diet is likely to cause anemia, which in some cases can slow down a child's development." The camera then shifts to the back seat, where a young boy, identified as Cuba, with curly hair, wearing a gray puffer jacket and blue jeans, sits in a car seat with a blue pacifier, looking around curiously. A man's voice, likely Sam Fish, speaks with a Welsh accent, expressing, "It's hitting home, the effect that his diet that we've allowed him to have is affecting him. Now he's got to have a blood test now, he could be anemic. It's obviously affecting his health. It does make me feel a bit guilty." The woman exits the car and walks briskly down a wet street, carrying Cuba, passing parked cars and brick buildings, arriving at a building with a blue awning labeled 'Meadow Sure Start Children’s Centre.' Inside, Cuba is seen playing with a colorful bead maze toy at a small table. The woman, now wearing a leopard-print coat, interacts with Cuba and a blonde woman in a white uniform, who is a nurse. The nurse, speaking gently and reassuringly, tells Cuba, "Right, okay, I'm just gonna pop this little strap on his arm, doesn't hurt, it's just a bit of pressure, okay? Just pop this on a minute, mate. Pull it nice and tight, okay? There we are. What a good boy. Now he shouldn't feel that, okay?" as she gently examines his arm, likely performing a vaccination or health screening. Cuba remains calm, still with the blue pacifier, in the bright, clean room with posters on the wall, including one that reads 'Calling all the people of Buxton!' and another about heart attack symptoms. The video ends with a close-up of Cuba’s face, eyes closed, pacifier in place, appearing peaceful and content during the medical procedure.

The video opens in a clinical setting where a healthcare professional, the Woman in white top, calmly administers an injection to a young child, identified as Cuba, a toddler with curly hair. Cuba, wearing a white shirt and blue jeans, cries and moves distressedly throughout the procedure. The Woman with dark hair, now wearing a leopard-print coat and identified as Sam, holds Cuba securely, comforting her with soft words like "Okay, darling" and questioning "What makes them so upset?" as Cuba's cries intensify. The nurse applies a bandage, and Sam carries the still-crying Cuba out of the room, walking down a hallway past signs for 'Dr. R.K. Tiwari Reception' and 'Cardiff & The Vale'. A narrator, revealed to be Sam herself, reflects on the event, stating, "That did not go well at all. It's traumatic for Cuba. It was traumatic for me," and expresses feeling "devastated now" as melancholic piano music begins to play over Cuba's continued crying. Sam's voice returns, explaining that "The results will come through in a couple of weeks." The scene transitions to an exterior shot of a red-brick house, then cuts to a bright, modern kitchen. Here, two young girls, one in a blue long-sleeve shirt and the other in a dark top, sit on a counter. One girl reaches into a cabinet for snacks, while the other watches and laughs, making a sound. Sam's voice is heard again, stating, "Back at home, Sam is determined to start changing her family's diet," followed by another adult female voice listing food items like "cereal, Weetabix, Cheerios, there's raisin..."

The video opens in a bright, modern kitchen with white cabinetry and black countertops, where a woman, identified by a narrator as Sam, in a gray sleeveless sweater and black pants, crouches to retrieve items from a lower cabinet. She then stands, gesturing and speaking in a British accent about food items like "raisins covered in yogurts" and "breadsticks," engaging in a conversation with a child who interjects, "Don't all believe in food." Sam responds, "Well, if you're hungry, that's what's there," and continues describing "oat biscuits," to which the child suggests, "You should get chocolate digestives." The scene briefly cuts to two young girls in a different kitchen setting—one in a dark top sitting on a counter, the other in a blue top laughing—before returning to Sam in the main kitchen. A narrator explains that Sam is "facing up to her fears of a messy kitchen" and has been "cooking fresh meals." Sam opens a refrigerator, revealing various food items, and then moves to a sink area where she handles a bowl of chopped vegetables. A man, identified by the narrator as Simon, in a black polo shirt appears, unpacking a large black bag on a kitchen island, placing items like a red container and a white tray inside. The narrator adds that "Simon is finding it hard to cut out the junk" and "yesterday, he was in charge of the kids." Sam, sounding frustrated, confronts Simon, stating, "No, you didn't make an effort, Simon. I was nearly in tears when I come home from work Friday. I was really, really deflated with you." Simon calmly interjects, "I was the first time," and later, "But I enjoyed my takeaway." Sam continues to argue about the realism of getting rid of "everything" and the responsibility for diet, concluding, "So we've got to take responsibility for that, Simon." As Sam continues her tasks at the sink, occasionally glancing toward Simon, gentle, reflective background music begins, underscoring the domestic and active atmosphere of meal preparation and family dynamics.

event_blocks.summary: In a warm, well-lit kitchen with gentle background music and cutlery sounds, a young child, Sam (also Tuba), with curly hair and wearing a gray and blue striped shirt, sits in a red high chair, eating vegetables, meatballs, and potatoes from a colorful plate with a fork and hands. A woman in a gray sleeveless sweater asks for "Fox," "cakes," and "potato," remarking, "I can't believe you eat it now" and "Goodbye, you are. Hey? Look at me tonight." A man in a black polo shirt observes, "Oh, look at that, go on, babe," and later, "Oh, no." A narrator states, "Sam's hard work is paying off," as the man notes, "He's enjoying it, eh? Tuba's actually eating for the first time ever," adding, "it's not chips, and it's not pepperamis, and it's literally been what, two days?" The woman then says, "Hang on, back for seconds," and the man asks, "And why not seconds, by J?" with the woman concluding, "Yeah, you're a growing man, aren't you? Just going that way," followed by laughter. The scene transitions to the family eating at a dining table, where the man unpacks a large black duffel-style bag. The setting shifts outdoors to a 'Welcome to RUNCORN TOWN CENTRE' sign, accompanied by upbeat, slightly melancholic instrumental music, with a narrator explaining, "In Runcorn, home-cooked meals are nowhere to be seen." The final scene depicts 19-year-old Taylor, in a pink top and black headband, sitting on a black leather couch, holding a KFC cup and applying makeup, while interacting with young Harley, who is in a blue sweater, eating fries with a blue pacifier in his mouth, and says "Water." The narrator concludes, "The less time 19-year-old Taylor spends cooking for Harley, the more..."

event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a close-up of a woman's feet in pink platform high-heeled boots on a wooden floor, accompanied by upbeat pop music. The scene shifts to a nighttime street where three women, including one in a pink top and black headband and another in a cream-colored sweater, walk and laugh before entering 'THE FERRY BOAT' pub. Inside, they discuss a child's eating habits, mentioning 'dippers and smileys,' 'cake,' and 'McDonald's,' with one suggesting they 'celebrate' and 'get smashed.' The setting changes to a daytime kitchen with pink and white appliances where a woman in a pink apron prepares food, placing banana slices on a tray. A young boy in a blue sweater, Harley, opens the refrigerator and reaches for snacks. The woman asks him about chicken dippers and instructs him to shut the drawer. A female voiceover reveals, "Three weeks into what's meant to be a new routine, three-year-old Harley..." Inside a living room, a woman in a pink top and black pants places a yellow divided plate with sausages and sauce on a black leather couch for Harley, who enthusiastically begins eating, explaining, "I cut my teeth up." Background music plays as a female narrator discusses dietitian Hayley Cooter and introduces "sugar-mad Harley" and an "immediate danger." Hayley Cooter, wearing a pink top and patterned headband, sits beside a baby in a pink and white checkered bouncer. The scene shifts outdoors to an alley beside a wooden fence where Hayley, in a black vest, talks to a woman in a green coat. Harley, in a black jacket and blue pacifier, stands between them. The woman in the green coat asks Harley if he's been to the dentist, to which he replies, "No, I'm scared of the dentist." They walk towards a 'CROSS DENTAL CENTRE' sign. The segment continues with Harley in a dental chair as a dentist inspects his teeth, explaining, 'very early signs of decay developing in the teeth,' attributing them to 'sugary drinks.' Taylor, observing, is told, 'Well done Harley.' Uplifting instrumental music begins as the narrator reveals it's a 'massive jolt for Taylor.' The scene transitions to Taylor and Harley walking down a city sidewalk, passing a 'Gillman Hill' sign, and entering a home. In a kitchen with wooden cabinets, Taylor speaks to the camera, expressing shock at the dentist's words and resolving to 'cut drinks out altogether other than water or milk.' Later, Taylor sits on a black leather couch, opening a green bag of snacks for Harley, who is wearing a black jacket and blue pacifier. The setting shifts to a living room with floral wallpaper, where Harley plays on the floor while Taylor watches him, as the narrator concludes, 'Taylor finally agrees to take expert advice on board and gets started.'

raw_segments: The video opens with a woman in a green coat and purple scarf standing outdoors in a residential area, visually speaking directly to the camera under an overcast sky, amidst terraced brick houses and trimmed hedges. A female narrator's voice discusses changing diets and the need for support groups for mothers in similar positions, mentioning 'Taylor' being inspired to attend 'Parent Power'. The scene transitions to a young woman in a white hoodie and blue jeans walking across a parking lot towards a brick building, identified by a sign as the 'HALTON YOUTH SERVICE Grangeway Youth Club'. Inside, a bright, active kitchen is bustling with several women in aprons preparing food, accompanied by light, uplifting background music. A woman with red hair and another with dark hair are seen assembling pizzas on a table, adding toppings like cheese, spinach, and mushrooms. A participant woman's voice explains that they meet every Friday afternoon to cook healthy alternatives to popular foods like pizzas, kebabs, and cakes. Another woman's voice interjects, "Just cook it all," followed by a man's voice admitting, "I've tried," and humorously adding, "Yeah, no, we set the house on fire." He continues, expressing his belief that he can't cook but would probably try if he did. The first participant woman's voice then details their diverse cooking, including spaghetti bolognese, roast dinners, and "healthy option pizzas." A close-up shows a young child with blonde hair and blue eyes, wearing a gray shirt, being held by an adult and eating, with food visible on their face, embodying the communal, nurturing atmosphere focused on cooking and child care within the youth service center. The man's voice concludes, "Looks like I just got out into me garden and decided."

The video opens in a vibrant, brightly lit kitchen or activity room at the Halton Youth Service Grangeway Youth Club, filled with background chatter and the sound of a woman saying, "make a pizza." A young boy in a blue and black checkered shirt is seen reaching for ingredients on a white table covered with food items, including cookies and a bowl of red sauce. A clear female narrator's voice explains that "Taylor's freshly prepared pizza contains around 470 calories," contrasting it with "Harley's usual delivered variety" which "would tot up a massive 3,000." The camera pans to show several women in black aprons with 'The Fun Food Chef' logo, assembling pizzas on white trays. One woman with dark hair and a black and white striped top carefully places toppings like cheese, spinach, mushrooms, and green onions onto a pizza base, while another woman with red hair, wearing a gray top and a black apron, also adds ingredients. The table is labeled with 'SUPPER • LUNCH • DINNER' and features bowls of fresh greens. Colorful posters, including 'Healthy Eating' and 'PARENT POWER', decorate the walls. The scene shifts to a close-up of a woman with dark hair and a braid, who speaks to the camera in a reflective voice, holding a piece of food. She shares her experience, stating she's been asked to come to different groups like this, where she feels people might judge her for being a "young single mum." She explains that before coming here, she "used to just order out" and would "sit eating like pasta and stuff like that." Another woman's voice briefly interjects, "Don't open it!" before the first woman continues, expressing that she "would have never actually sat and thought, yeah, let's try this." She adds that she'll "definitely come again" because it's "something I can take home with me," allowing her and "Harley" to "actually sit and say, 'Oh, I made my tea, I made this.'" Another voice confirms, "You made yours!" The woman with the braid concludes that it will "bring us closer together in a healthy way." The video then transitions to an outdoor shot of a suburban neighborhood with terraced brick houses under a blue sky with scattered clouds, as melancholic, jazzy acoustic guitar music begins to play. The final scene shows a woman in a black top and polka-dot skirt placing a baby in a gray shirt onto a black leather couch in a living room filled with toys and a colorful play tent, while a child's voice sings, "You can't eat some breakfast," followed by a woman saying, "Okay," and the narrator concluding with "Inside..."

event_blocks.summary: Kara, dressed in a black top and polka-dot skirt, attempts to feed her 19-month-old son, Michael, who is wearing a gray onesie, placing him on a black leather couch and offering a green sippy cup, while expressing concern over his uneaten toast and the mess he is making. The narrator explains that Michael's parents have become overcontrolling at mealtimes since he contracted meningitis a year ago, leading to his rebellion, a situation contrasted by brief scenes of Gareth, in a checkered shirt, clapping while eating at a dining table and later pushing a stroller with a child on a suburban sidewalk. As upbeat, whimsical music begins, the narrator reveals Michael has been referred to feeding expert Lucy Thomas, who, in a brightly lit children’s center with checkered flooring and posters of 'Beetroot' and 'yellow pepper', kneels to interact with Michael, now in a gray sweater and blue bib, playfully offering him a cucumber and encouraging him to take it to his father, Gareth, who watches and smiles, dressed in a black leather jacket. Lucy Thomas explains her classes focus on allowing parents and children to explore and experiment with food, emphasizing sensory experiences and making mealtimes fun, as Michael walks toward her and Gareth gently guides him, with Lucy exclaiming, "Fantastic!" The scene transitions to the children's center where Michael, wearing a blue bib, is encouraged by the woman in red to pick a beetroot, and praised for taking it back to his father, with the narrator noting that "Without the stress and pressure of his usual meal times, Michael becomes adventurous with the fruit and veg." In a warm dining area, Michael, along with other children, sits at a low table with colorful plastic bowls and plates, where a man in a black and white striped shirt feeds Michael apple slices and helps him with a green sippy cup, while the woman in red leads a playful song about stirring soup. The video then shows Michael being fed apple slices by his father, Gareth, in a black and white striped shirt, with Gareth enthusiastically describing Michael's positive experience at an activity, stating Michael "really took to it really well" and will be attending weekly. Gareth, now in a black jacket and patterned scarf, pushes Michael, in a brown hooded coat, in a stroller outdoors as upbeat acoustic guitar music plays, followed by scenes of a sunset and a brick house. Back home, Gareth, in a gray shirt, tries to bring the "classroom fun into the home," assisting Michael, now in a high chair with sliced cucumbers and beetroot, and a sippy cup, encouraging him to eat with him, though Michael responds with a soft vocalization and a distinct "No" when offered more food, leading Gareth to playfully question his dislike, referencing his previous enjoyment. The interaction takes place in a living space with a black leather couch, a white desk with a computer, and framed photos on the window ledge.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a narrator introducing Kara and Gareth, who are trying to feed their 19-month-old son, Michael, healthy home-cooked food. Visually, Kara, in a black top and polka-dot skirt, places Michael, wearing a gray onesie, onto a black leather couch, adjusting cushions before offering him a green sippy cup. Kara's voice expresses concern, noting, "He hasn't touched his toast," and later, "Look at this mess, look at this mess, Michael," as she tries to encourage him to eat "nona." The narrator explains that since contracting meningitis a year ago, Michael's parents have become overcontrolling at mealtimes, leading to Michael's rebellion. The scene briefly shifts to Gareth, in a checkered shirt, clapping as he eats at a dining table, then to him pushing a stroller with a child on a suburban sidewalk. Upbeat, whimsical music begins as the narrator reveals Michael has been referred to feeding expert Lucy Thomas for practical support for Kara and Gareth. The video transitions to a brightly lit children’s center, where Lucy Thomas, in a red long-sleeve top, dances energetically before kneeling to interact with Michael, now in a gray sweater and blue bib, engaging him playfully with a cucumber. Gareth, in a black leather jacket, watches and smiles. Lucy Thomas's clear voice explains her classes are about offering parents with young children the opportunity to explore and experiment with fruit and vegetables, emphasizing the importance of feeling and smelling food rather than just putting it straight into their mouth, making it a fun and positive experience for Michael. The scene concludes with Lucy clapping as Michael walks toward her, and Gareth gently guiding him while Lucy continues her enthusiastic encouragement, exclaiming, "Fantastic!" The center's walls display educational posters like 'Beetroot' and 'yellow pepper', and colorful bowls of produce are visible on the checkered floor.

The video opens in a brightly lit children’s center, characterized by checkered flooring and colorful walls adorned with fruit and vegetable posters, including 'Beetroot' and 'yellow pepper'. A young child, identified by a nurturing female voice as Michael, wearing a blue bib with a green cartoon character, walks across the room toward a woman in a red long-sleeve top. The woman, speaking with an enthusiastic tone, calls out, "Michael, Michael, do you want to come and pick a beetroot?" as she kneels near a green curtain, engaging him with a cucumber and encouraging him to "Take it back to Daddy, Michael!" praising his efforts with "Well done, Michael!". She also playfully instructs other children to "brush their teeth." A narrator's voice then provides context, stating that "Without the stress and pressure of his usual meal times, Michael becomes adventurous with the fruit and veg." The scene shifts to a warm dining area where Michael and several other children are seated at a low table with colorful plastic bowls and plates. A man in a black and white striped shirt feeds Michael a piece of apple and helps him use a green sippy cup. The woman in red, now seated and smiling, leads the children in a playful song, "Stir, stir, stir the soup, we stir it round and round," encouraging them to make "slurping sounds" which she praises as "lovely soup slurping." Other children, including one with curly hair in a blue bib, are also eating cucumber slices and other snacks. The atmosphere is warm and nurturing, with adults actively assisting children in eating and engaging them in playful learning, all within an environment reinforced by educational posters, a blue bulletin board, and natural light from large windows.

The video opens with a close-up of Michael, an infant in a blue bib with a green cartoon character, being fed apple slices by his father, Gareth, who wears a black and white striped shirt. A narrator introduces Michael's 'mini triumph' as Gareth enthusiastically describes an activity Michael attended as 'brilliant,' stating Michael 'really took to it really well' and will be 'coming every week now.' The scene transitions to an outdoor setting where Gareth, now in a black jacket and patterned scarf, pushes Michael in a stroller, who wears a brown hooded coat. Upbeat acoustic guitar music begins to play as the camera captures a dramatic sunset, followed by a clear blue sky over a brick house. Inside, the narrator explains that Gareth is trying to bring the 'classroom fun into the home.' Gareth, in a gray shirt, makes a 'Woo!' sound before assisting Michael, now in a high chair with sliced cucumbers and beetroot, placing a sippy cup on the table. Gareth encourages Michael, asking, 'You're going to eat it with Daddy now, yeah?' accompanied by tapping sounds. Michael, wearing a gray jacket and green bib, is then seen standing on a blue rug in a living room, playing with toys, before being picked up and carried by Gareth. Gareth continues to encourage Michael, saying, 'You're gonna come back, have some more. Come on, please, come on.' Michael responds with a soft vocalization and a distinct 'No' when offered more food. Gareth playfully makes an 'Mmm-mmm' sound and asks, 'When you brush your teeth?' before commenting, 'You don't like any of it. You did the other day,' with a questioning 'Hmm?' The interaction is warm and playful, set against a homey living space with a black leather couch, a white desk with a computer, and framed photos on the window ledge.

event_blocks.summary: In a warm living room, Gareth, a man in a gray button-up shirt, attempts to feed his young son, Michael, a slice of cucumber from a white plate of sliced vegetables, but Michael spits it out, leading Gareth to express his own disinterest and lack of confidence in the food. Michael then drinks from a green sippy cup, guided by Gareth, but begins to cry loudly, highlighting the ongoing marital strain and chaotic mealtimes. The scene shifts to a kitchen where Michael peers into a sink cabinet filled with cleaning supplies while Gareth smiles at the camera, and Kara, in a denim shirt, kneels by a washing machine interacting with Michael, as the narrator announces their upcoming consultation with Dr. Katherine. Outdoors, Gareth and Kara walk hand-in-hand to a cozy pub, where they sit at a wooden table. Gareth, in a black sweater, and Kara, in a leopard-print scarf, discuss their worries about Michael not reaching his milestones and Kara expresses fear when he doesn't eat enough. Later, in a pub with large windows overlooking a snowy scene, a woman in a purple top, a woman in a black top with a leopard-print scarf, and a man in a black sweater engage in a lively conversation about someone getting thinner and hungry, and whether children are reaching their milestones, concluding they are too quick to stress. Playfully, the woman in purple lifts the table, revealing the others crouched underneath. A woman with dark hair and a black top then explains to the camera that children use all their senses to assess food and announces a fun game. In a spacious room with hardwood floors, the woman in purple leads a tasting activity at a wooden table, explaining it simulates how Michael feels about unknown food. Participants, including the man in the black sweater and the woman in the leopard-print scarf, smell and taste samples from aluminum cups on a black circular tray, with some samples being inedible. They laugh and decline to eat a sample that smells 'foody,' which is revealed to be an iron tonic, confirming its irony in smell and taste. The woman in purple then poses a hypothetical to Michael about being told to eat something beneficial.

raw_segments: The video opens in a warm living room where Gareth, a man in a gray button-up shirt, sits on the floor with his young son, Michael, who wears a gray hoodie and a green bib. Gareth offers Michael a slice of cucumber from a white plate of sliced apples, cucumbers, and red peppers, saying "have a bite." Michael makes a "buh" sound and sighs, prompting Gareth to remark to Kara, "this isn't going down well," and "He's spat nearly every bit out." Kara responds, "Are you joking?" A narrator explains that "Michael's making small changes, but his parents are not." Gareth expresses his disinterest in the food, stating, "I'm not keen on that anyway," and that it's a "matter of confidence for me doing it as well." Michael then drinks from a green sippy cup, guided by Gareth, but soon begins to cry loudly. The narrator adds that "Kara and Gareth's relationship is still suffering, and mealtimes are still chaos." Kara asks Michael, "Do you want to go upstairs to bed?" while Gareth encourages him, "Come on, be a good boy." The scene transitions to a kitchen where Michael, now standing, peers into a sink cabinet, revealing cleaning supplies, while Gareth, in a green shirt, smiles at the camera. Kara, with dark hair tied back and wearing a denim shirt, kneels by a washing machine to interact with Michael. The narrator then states, "Thankfully, their first consultation with Dr. Katherine is scheduled." The setting shifts outdoors to a sidewalk where Gareth and Kara, dressed in winter coats, walk hand-in-hand before entering a cozy pub. Seated at a wooden table, Gareth in a black sweater and Kara in a leopard-print scarf, they engage in a serious conversation. Kara asks, "What particularly is it that that worries you?" Gareth responds, expressing concern that Michael "is not going to reach his milestones and maybe miss out on things and be a bit slow." Kara concludes, "I get really scared when he doesn't eat enough."

The minute opens in a warmly lit pub, featuring large windows overlooking a snowy outdoor scene, where three individuals are seated around a rustic wooden table. The Woman in purple top, with short blonde hair, speaks animatedly, discussing someone getting "thinner and thinner" and "so hungry," going to "starve," calling it "silly." Beside her, the Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf, wearing a black headband and gold hoop earrings, smiles and gestures, while the Man in black sweater, with curly light brown hair, laughs, covering his mouth. The Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf then reflects on being "right there with the emotion" when someone was ill. The Man in black sweater, who later holds a small object between his fingers, agrees, stating one tends to "feel worse," prompting further agreement from the women. The Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf questions if someone is "unhappy" and if they "look at those got energy," to which the Man in black sweater responds with a "Yeah" and a laugh. She continues, asking if they are "reaching his milestones." The Man in black sweater then remarks on their silliness, saying "we know he's all this is..." before the Woman in purple top interjects, "We know he's okay, like." The Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf concludes this segment by suggesting they are "too quick to get stressed" and "start fretting." Throughout this lively interaction, the camera pans and zooms between them. Playfully, the Woman in purple top then stands and lifts the table, revealing the Man in black sweater and the Woman in black top with leopard-print scarf crouched underneath. The scene then cuts to a close-up of a Woman with dark hair and black top, in a different setting with patterned wallpaper, who speaks directly to the camera. Her clear, informative narration, accompanied by gentle background music, explains that "Children are naturally suspicious of food and use all their senses to work out what's okay to eat," before she announces, "We're going to play a game together, and I hope it's going to be fun." The overall atmosphere is casual, humorous, and social.

The video opens in a warmly lit, spacious room with hardwood floors, where a woman in a purple top, with short blonde hair and glasses, leads a tasting activity at a rustic wooden table. She speaks animatedly, explaining in a clear, articulate voice that the activity is about experiencing how 'Michael feels' when presented with unknown food, and hopes 'Kara and Gareth' (the woman in the leopard-print scarf and the man in the black sweater, respectively) will understand. She then warns them that 'some of them are not edible,' prompting the man in the black sweater to express concern, stating, 'It's quite worrying when you say some of them aren't edible.' The camera cuts to close-ups of hands placing small aluminum cups with dark liquids onto a black circular tray. The participants smell and taste the samples, with thoughtful, curious, and amused expressions. The woman in purple asks, 'So would you eat that?' to which both the man and the woman in the scarf respond with laughter and a firm 'No, I wouldn't eat that.' The woman in the scarf comments that one sample 'smells foody.' Laughter fills the convivial atmosphere as they continue tasting. The woman in purple then puts them 'out of their misery,' revealing the liquid is 'an iron tonic.' The woman in the scarf confirms, 'It does smell irony and taste a bit irony,' with the man agreeing. The woman in purple concludes by setting up a hypothetical scenario for 'Michael,' asking what he would do if told to 'eat that, it's good for you.'

event_blocks.summary: In a warmly lit room with hardwood floors, a woman in a purple top, wearing glasses, leads a tasting activity at a wooden dining table, explaining to participants, including a woman in a black top with a leopard-print scarf, that children need "16 tastes" and naturally "reach out, touch, feel, and do some squishing" with new foods, which is "not bad manners." The woman in the leopard-print scarf audibly reacts with "Wow" after smelling and tasting from aluminum cups on a black circular tray, while a man's voice is heard laughing. The scene shifts to a car interior where "Dad Simon," driving with a British accent, discusses sneaking off to the cake shop on Fridays with his girls, accompanied by the hum of the car engine and road noise. Beside him, a child named "Cuba," with curly hair and a blue pacifier, sits in a car seat. The car navigates a suburban street lined with terraced houses, passing under a large arched bridge in the warm, late afternoon sunlight, with upbeat background music playing.

raw_segments: The video opens in a warmly lit room with hardwood floors, where three individuals are seated around a wooden dining table. A woman with short blonde hair, wearing glasses and a purple long-sleeve top, actively leads a tasting activity, gesturing as she speaks about a child's natural inclination to "reach out, touch, feel, and do some squishing" with new foods, emphasizing that "that's not bad manners." Another woman, wearing a black top with a leopard-print scarf, participates, smelling and tasting from small aluminum cups on a black circular tray, and audibly reacts with "Wow" to the information. A man's voice is heard laughing in response to the discussion. The scene then transitions to a car interior, where the man, identified by a narrator as "Dad Simon," is driving. He speaks in a British accent about being "a bit naughty now" and how he "always treat my girls, especially on a Friday, we always sneak off to the cake shop," accompanied by the hum of the car engine and road noise. Beside him, a young child, identified as "Cuba," with curly hair and a blue pacifier, sits curiously in a car seat. The car travels through a suburban street with terraced houses and a large arched bridge under the warm, late afternoon sunlight, while upbeat background music plays.

event_blocks.summary: In a suburban street scene at golden hour, Simon, wearing a red and blue jacket, tells girls he will "treat them with some cakes," and a female narrator reveals he is "sneaking in snacks behind Sam's back" as he pushes a stroller into a store. Simon asks the girls, "But we don't tell Mummy, do we, girls?" and a child replies, "No." As a woman in a black jacket passes a bakery display, Simon asks, "What cookies are we having?" and a girl excitedly shouts, "Cake, cake, cake, yeah." After confirming their choices, Simon urges, "Come on, let's get back to the car quick." Inside the car, a young child with curly hair in a gray puffer jacket holds a wooden toy in a car seat, while a woman in a pink and white striped shirt sits nearby. Simon asks, "Enjoying that?" and mentions his own treat, "I got a chocolate cake with cream I have. I can't wait." Birds are silhouetted on a rooftop against a sunset sky. The scene shifts to a bright kitchen with white cabinets, where Simon, now in a gray hoodie, is on the phone, discussing food and saying he "changed the chicken tikka for another lamb pathanda piece." Upbeat background music begins as a female narrator explains, "Back at home, there's more fast food on the menu, as the Fish family are tucking into their one weekly takeaway." A woman in a red top unpacks bread and eggs from a blue bag, while a young girl in a red school uniform and another in a green top eat and help prepare food. A child says, "Happy birthday," and Simon offers, "Right, let's get you some more chips. Yes, more chips for all week." The narrator clarifies the takeaway is "a reward for all their good work." Simon remarks, "We haven't had one of these for ages now, we girls, since Jane's come along," and a child responds, "No." The girls continue to eat and interact, with the woman in green placing a plate of food on the counter. Later, a close-up shows a hand serving fried food onto rice in a kitchen, with a bottle of 'Thena' sauce visible. A young child, Cooper, with curly hair and an orange shirt, eats fries from a pink plate in a red high chair, making an 'Mmm' sound as a woman's voice says, 'yum yum yummy in Cooper's tum,' with a sippy cup nearby. The woman in the red long-sleeve top and black jacket unpacks groceries, including bread and eggs from a blue bag, while a young girl in a red school uniform and another in a green top assist. A man in a dark 'SHINJU' hoodie, who was "looking forward to this," sits at the counter eating. Cooper is shown happily eating with his hands. Upbeat, dramatic background music plays as a female narrator discusses a "secret cake raid" and a male narrator states, "Daddy's trying his best." The scene transitions to an exterior shot of the suburban street at golden hour, where the woman in red, carrying a black container and the blue bag, walks toward a brick house with a white front door, entering through the doorway.
