raw_segments: The video opens with a serene suburban street scene at golden hour, featuring cars driving past leafless trees and terraced houses. The setting then shifts to a grocery store where Simon, an adult man in a red and blue jacket, is heard saying he will "treat them with some cakes." A female narrator reveals that "Simon is sneaking in snacks behind Sam's back" as he pushes a stroller into the store. Simon then asks the girls, "But we don't tell Mummy, do we, girls?" to which a child's voice responds, "No." As a woman in a black jacket walks past a bakery display featuring 'flake fancy' priced at 53p, Simon asks, "What cookies are we having?" and a girl excitedly replies, "Cake, cake, cake, yeah." Simon continues to interact with the girls about their choices, asking "Which ones do you want? You got everything?" and a child confirms, "Yeah," before he urges, "Come on, let's get back to the car quick." Inside a car, a young child with curly hair in a gray puffer jacket is seen in a car seat, holding a wooden toy, while a woman in a pink and white striped shirt sits beside them. Simon's voice is heard asking, "Enjoying that?" and then describing his own treat, "I got a chocolate cake with cream I have. I can't wait." A peaceful shot of birds silhouetted on a rooftop against a warm sunset sky follows. The scene transitions to a bright, domestic kitchen with white cabinets, a fruit basket, and floral curtains, where Simon, now in a gray hoodie with yellow text, is on the phone. Upbeat, slightly jazzy background music begins as Simon's voice is heard discussing food, saying he "changed the chicken tikka for another lamb pathanda piece." A female narrator explains, "Back at home, there's more fast food on the menu, as the Fish family are tucking into their one weekly takeaway." A woman in a red top unpacks groceries, placing bread and eggs from a blue bag onto the counter, while a young girl in a red school uniform eats at the table. Another young girl in a green top joins her, helping prepare food. A child's voice is heard saying "Happy birthday," and Simon offers, "Right, let's get you some more chips. Yes, more chips for all week." The narrator clarifies that the takeaway "is meant to be there as a reward for all their good work." Simon then remarks, "We haven't had one of these for ages now, we girls, since Jane's come along," to which a child responds, "No," before Simon's voice is cut off saying, "Jane's..." The girls continue to eat and interact, with the woman in green placing a plate of food on the counter, reinforcing the theme of shared family life.

The video opens with a close-up of a hand using a fork to serve golden fried food, identified as 'takeaways' by a lively woman's voice, onto a plate of rice in a warmly lit kitchen, where a bottle of 'Thena' brand sauce is visible. The scene shifts to a young child, named Cooper, with curly hair and an orange shirt, happily seated in a red high chair with a patterned backrest, eating fries from a pink plate with a blue spoon, making an 'Mmm' sound of enjoyment as the woman's voice affectionately says, 'yum yum yummy in Cooper's tum.' A young girl in a red school uniform and another in a green top assist with serving food, while a man in a dark 'SHINJU' hoodie, heard stating in a calm, conversational tone that he was 'looking forward to this,' sits at the counter, eating and engaging in conversation with expressive gestures. The family interacts warmly, with the woman in green placing food on plates and the man gesturing as he speaks. The young child, Cooper, is shown in close-up, happily eating with his hands. Throughout the segment, an upbeat, slightly dramatic background music plays, accompanying a female narrator from a TV show discussing a 'secret cake raid' and later a male narrator stating, 'Daddy's trying his best,' before the female narrator continues about a 'health visitor's' second visit. The scene transitions to an exterior shot of a suburban street at golden hour, where the woman in red, carrying a black container and the blue bag, walks toward a brick house with a white front door, entering through the doorway, with the warm sunlight casting long shadows.

event_blocks.summary: In a modern kitchen, a man in a gray t-shirt, Simon, is playfully challenged by two women, one in a black cardigan over a yellow top and another in a red top with a colorful scarf, to give up his favorite fast food, specifically fried chicken and chips, with the women teasing him about 'gristle' and 'tempura chicken with water.' Simon, laughing, grabs a blue plastic bag with a rustling sound and leaves the kitchen, reminded to 'Don't forget the beans, babe.' He is later seen driving a blue car with the blue bag and a white plastic bag, accompanied by upbeat music. The scene then shifts to the kitchen where the women unpack the blue bag, revealing golden fries and fried chicken pieces placed on a plate. Simon, now seated at the counter, takes a large bite of chicken, but the women critique its greasy appearance and quantity, with one noting '16 pieces of chicken' and another exclaiming 'Oh, gross,' demonstrating how grease can be squeezed out and rivers of grease running down a hand, concluding it's 'food gorged in harmful fat' and 'should not be your daily diet.' Later, in a child’s bedroom with floral wallpaper, Jane, in the red top and black cardigan, searches for hidden snacks for 'Cuba,' placing items and a blue cup into a teal trash bin, asking 'What's all the contraband we got here?' A young child, Cuba, in a green vest, is briefly seen near a staircase with ornate black railings. Jane continues her tidying, eliciting laughter and exclamations like 'Oh my word!' as rustling and crinkling sounds are heard throughout the domestic, family-oriented scene.

raw_segments: The video opens with a low-angle shot of a bright, modern kitchen, focusing on a metal fruit basket in the foreground while blurring a man and two women conversing in the background. The camera then shifts to a medium shot, revealing the man, Simon, in a gray graphic t-shirt, seated on a stool, engaged in a lively discussion with two women. One woman, in a black cardigan over a yellow top, sits on the counter, while the other, in a red top and black cardigan with a colorful scarf, stands beside her. The women playfully challenge Simon to buy his favorite fast food, asking what he's "most missing from your diet" and then teasing him about "fried chicken and chips" and how they'll try to "put you off it" with "home truths" and talk of "gristle." Simon, laughing, confidently asserts he won't be deterred, even when reminded about "tempura chicken with water." As one woman tells him "On your way then," Simon stands, grabs a blue plastic bag from the counter with a distinct rustling sound, and exits the kitchen, with a final playful reminder from a woman, "Don't forget the beans, babe." The scene cuts to the exterior of a terraced brick house, then to Simon driving a blue car, holding the blue bag and a white plastic bag, accompanied by upbeat, rhythmic background music and the sound of the car driving. A narrator concludes, stating "Simon reunites himself with a super..." as the segment ends.

The video opens with a close-up of Simon, a man in a gray t-shirt with a graphic print, driving and holding a piece of fried food, as a voice-over introduces a "large size bucket of fried chicken in lightning quick time." Simon remarks, "I think this is gonna be really hard giving up this," and confirms he "couldn't resist" when prompted by a woman's voice. The scene transitions to a bright, modern kitchen where a woman in a black cardigan over a yellow top and another woman in a red top with a black cardigan and colorful scarf are unpacking a blue plastic bag, pouring golden fries and placing fried chicken pieces onto a white paper-lined plate. As Simon, now seated at the counter, takes a large bite of the chicken, the woman in the yellow top asks if it's "appealing," to which Simon replies it "smells nice," though the woman in the red top interjects that it "doesn't smell nice to me, it smells very very greasy really." They discuss the quantity, with the woman in the yellow top identifying him as "Simon" and noting "16 pieces of chicken," questioning if he'd normally have bought "this amount." The woman in the yellow top then exclaims, "Oh, gross," and encourages, "Look, you can actually squeeze it out." As one of the women breaks open a piece of fried chicken, revealing its juicy interior and crispy texture, the woman in the red top points out the grease "bubbling to the surface." Simon agrees it's "pretty gross," and the woman in the yellow top describes "Rivers of grease running down my hand," concluding, "This should not be your daily diet." Somber music then begins as a narrator states, "This is food gorged in harmful fat," while the kitchen, illuminated by warm under-cabinet lighting and featuring a stainless steel range hood, maintains a relaxed, domestic atmosphere centered on the food and the group's critical discussion.

The video opens in a bright, modern kitchen where three adults are gathered around a white countertop. A woman, identified by a narrator as Jane, wearing a red top, black cardigan, and colorful scarf, actively explains something, asking "Exactly does that mean for Cuba?" and later commenting "Gross, isn't it?" as she discusses the "amount of saturated fat that Cuba was getting in his diet every week," which she deems "really bad news" and a "heart attack diet." The man in a gray t-shirt with a graphic print and a woman in a black cardigan over a yellow floral top listen intently, with the woman in the black cardigan agreeing with a "Yeah" and adding, "We've had this like three times a week, one week, haven't we? Plus then we've had ever takeaways and top of this." The man in the gray t-shirt chimes in with "maybe more" and "Don't look so tasty, does it?" Jane responds, "Be fried, isn't it?" as a close-up shows a plate of golden fried food, likely fish and chips. Throughout this, sounds of rustling and crinkling are heard, possibly from food packaging. The scene transitions to a child’s bedroom with floral wallpaper, where Jane is tidying up. A narrator explains that "Jane wants to make sure sisters Cleo and Shay aren't hiding away any snacks for a foraging Cuba." Jane is seen placing snacks and a blue cup into a teal trash bin, asking "What's all the contraband we got here?" and exclaiming "Wow!" as more rustling sounds accompany her actions. A young child, referred to as Cuba, in a green vest and gray pants, is briefly seen playing with a red balloon near a staircase with ornate black railings. Jane continues her search, asking "Anything under the pillow?" which elicits laughter, and then "And what's in the bin?" before exclaiming "Oh my word!" as she continues tidying, with more rustling and laughter heard, emphasizing the domestic, family-oriented atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: In a child's bedroom with floral wallpaper, a young girl in a black top sits with a laptop while another girl in a pink top lies beside her. The scene shifts to a kitchen counter displaying a metal fruit basket, then to a red front door where a woman, Jane, in a red top and black cardigan enters carrying a blue bag. She ascends a staircase with ornate black railings into a child's bedroom with floral wallpaper, discarding items into a teal trash bin as a narrator notes her focus on "the whole family's future health" and that "Old habits are hard to break." In another kitchen scene, a woman in a pink top and black headband arranges banana slices on a tray, accompanied by upbeat acoustic music. The video then shows a suburban street with terraced brick houses, including a shop named 'FISH & FINS'. Inside a home, feeding expert Lucy Thomas, in a green hoodie, and a man in a black sweater interact with a young child, Michael, in a gray sweater, who babbles and cries softly. Michael plays on a colorful vegetable-themed rug, picks up broccoli, and Lucy encourages him to "show Michael how you can break the broccoli." The family moves to the kitchen, where Lucy continues to engage Michael with vegetables, explaining it's not just about "something that's put down there and oh, it's time to start eating." Michael exclaims "Ta!" and Lucy responds "Wow!" before offering him a bowl of broccoli to hold, which he declines, so she suggests giving it to "Mummy to cook" as a woman in a denim jacket observes. Later, in a bright kitchen, the Woman in green hoodie encourages Michael, holding a yellow bowl of broccoli, to "shake them" and asks, "What do you want the broccoli for? Is it because it's your broccoli?" Michael giggles, and the Woman in denim jacket and black dress guides him. Michael, in a patterned gray sweater and blue jeans, plays with the broccoli, dropping it occasionally, with the Woman in green hoodie retrieving it and stating, "That's for Michael," before suggesting they "go and take it and show Daddy." The scene moves to a living room with a black leather couch and a colorful vegetable-themed rug, where Michael plays with vegetables like a large carrot under the supervision of the Woman in green hoodie, while the Woman in denim jacket and black dress sits on the floor interacting with him. A narrator explains that "Michael is continuing to make small changes every day," and that "Car and Gareth are much more relaxed with his progress." Upbeat, gentle instrumental music plays as the setting transitions to an outdoor suburban street with terraced brick houses, where a man (likely Gareth) and woman (likely Car) walk together, the man carrying Michael. Psychologist Dr. Catherine Dendy is then mentioned for a "final check on Michael." The video concludes with a close-up of a white bowl of steamed broccoli and a "dip, dip, dip" sound.

event_blocks.summary: A woman with dark hair, wearing a black lace top and leopard-print headband, reflects on her health and past mistakes, stating, "I need to start eating healthy because of me heart," and a desire to avoid repeating errors "for the kids." Outdoors on stone steps, she discusses a past "lack of motivation and just laziness," but now feels empowered, asserting, "I know that I can do things." Back inside, she asks a young child, "Harley, which pizza's nicer?" and the child replies, "This one." Upbeat music begins as a blonde woman with glasses holds a baby in a pink outfit, softly calling it "Cuba" and saying, "Come on, lovely." After an exterior shot of a red-brick house, a woman in a yellow top tucks a child into a crib in a bedroom with soccer-ball wallpaper. A narrator announces, "In Cardiff, Cuba's blood test for anemia has finally come." In the child's bedroom, Sam, in a yellow top and floral skirt, tucks her son, Cuba, into a crib with soccer-patterned bedding; Cuba wears a white polka-dot shirt and blue jeans. The narrator explains someone was "severely anemic" and it was addressed by changing their "old diet." Sam playfully lifts Cuba out. The scene shifts to a bright kitchen where the narrator introduces "The Fish family," who have "totally changed their eating habits," with Simon curbing fast food cravings and Sam excelling at home cooking. Sam states, "I'm cooking every day now, to be honest with you, which is not too bad." Simon, in a striped polo shirt, washes dishes at the sink while boiling potatoes and carrots steam on the stove. Sam chops green vegetables. The narrator adds that "Cuba's new diet is improving his sleep patterns" and he's "well on the way to beating his anemia." Cuba is brought into the kitchen, and the family moves to the backyard. Simon opens a trampoline net, and Cuba jumps inside, exclaiming, "Mama, hello! Whoa!" Simon explains that "what we were giving him before with the fried chicken and stuff like that was way too much for him" and they "didn't think it was affecting him as much as it was." The backyard has a clothesline and neighboring houses. A young child with curly hair runs near a trampoline as a male voice says, "When you actually see it for yourself then... you're gonna change that." The narrator mentions "Sam" and her "messy kitchen." In the kitchen, a man in a striped polo shirt washes potatoes in soapy water, exclaiming, "Look at that! I got to make a mess. What did I say to you, right? That will get washed up." The narrator states, "the whole family and of course Cuba, are on the path to a healthier future." The man cooks boiling potatoes and steaming carrots, then serves a meal to a child in a high chair. The child eats from a plate with a fork as the man pours a brown sauce from a red measuring cup over mashed potatoes, broccoli, carrots, and meat. A woman in a cream-colored sweater on a couch reflects, "Looking back at it all now, it was nothing to do with Cuba, absolutely nothing. So the problem definitely laid with us... that we didn't take control of, control of our baby, basically. He was controlling us, so it was definitely, definitely with us." Upbeat, rhythmic music with synth elements plays as the video ends with a black screen displaying white text production credits, including 'Narrator Hannah Peckham' and 'Filmed, Produced & Directed by Vicki Cooper', and a pink 'ORIGIN' logo.
